input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. In the CAD views, the front hoop is highlighted in pink and the front hoop braces are highlighted in blue. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule F.6.3.4 specified in the FSAE rule document? To answer the question, use the scale bar shown at the top of the engineering drawing to compute necessary dimensions in the drawing.  First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.6.3.4
            TITLE: the front hoop braces must be attached as near as possible to the top of the front hoop but

            CONTENT:
            rule F.6.3.4: The Front Hoop Braces must be attached as near as possible to the top of the Front Hoop but
F.6.3.4 The Front Hoop Braces must be attached as near as possible to the top of the Front Hoop but 
not more than 50 mm below the top-most surface of the Front Hoop.  See figure following

final_answer:
Explanation: Rule F.6.3.4 mandates that the Front Hoop Braces must be attached as near as possible to the top of the Front Hoop, but not more than 50 mm below the top-most surface of the Front Hoop. By examining the CAD views, specifically the front-facing views, the blue highlighted Front Hoop Braces are visibly attached to the pink highlighted Front Hoop. The vertical distance between the top surface of the Front Hoop and the attachment points of the braces appears to be significantly less than 50 mm, thus complying with the maximum allowable separation specified by the rule.

Answer: Yes
